design_philosophy.core_loop: Aim and shoot incoming enemy spacecraft, bombs, missiles, and asteroids while preserving shield health and avoiding friendly vessels
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Precision aiming under time pressure with degrading shield as penalty for missed shots
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Defend Earth from alien invasion by piloting defensive position against overwhelming odds across the Solar System
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unforgiving aiming-based difficulty with constant shield degradation creates high mechanical execution demands; friendly-fire mechanic adds strategic consideration to reflexive shooting

difficulty.lives_system: Shield-based health system that degrades with each missed shot; depleted shield results in failure
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based progression through Solar System locations (Uranus, Saturn, Jupiter, Earth) with boss encounters at level conclusions
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited agency; difficulty appears fixed and non-adjustable. Player can manage resource expenditure (bomb usage, power-up collection) but cannot reduce incoming attack speed

progression.upgrade_system: Temporary power-ups: bomb (screen-clear), comet (temporary invulnerability for limited hits), shield-repair item from friendly spacecraft
progression.unlock_mechanism: Linear level progression through planets: Uranus → Saturn → Jupiter → Earth, boss gate at each stage
replayability_hooks: Extreme difficulty encourages skill mastery and repeated attempts
High score potential from precision aiming

world_building_techniques: Briefing text: 2050 setting with Ragossian alien invasion using bioweapon
Mission framing: retrieve antibacterial medicine from Triton base to Earth
Level geography: progression through Solar System creates sense of journey
Final boss character: Nemesis (alien antagonist blocking Earth's atmosphere)
Victory acknowledgment: congratulation from Nester, president of terrestrial federation

boss_design.philosophy: Single major antagonist (Nemesis) serves as final level gatekeeper; boss fights conclude each level as environmental escalation point
notable_bosses: Nemesis - Final boss blocking Earth's atmosphere; represents culmination of invasion defense objective

danger_signals: Constant alien ship intrusion through planetary barriers
Time pressure emphasized in mission briefing
environmental_storytelling: Solar System geography creates spatial narrative of invasion cordon
Friendly vs. enemy ship distinction requires player-side identification
Shield degradation mechanic visualizes defensive strain
